cards.back: A 2:1 multiplexer is a basic digital circuit that selects one of three input signals and passes it to the output, based on a select input signal.
A 4:1 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of four input signals and passes it to the output, based on a select input signal.
An 8:1 multiplexer is a digital circuit that selects one of eight input signals and passes it to the output, based on a select input signal.
A D flip-flop is a type of digital circuit that stores a binary digit (0 or 1) in a memory cell, and can be used to implement counters, shift registers, and other digital circuits.
An encoder is a digital circuit that takes a binary input and produces a coded output, often used in applications such as serial communication and data encoding.
